notes: Behavioral evals for the remediated skill. Each case stages a minimal sanctum (evals/sanctum/*) so the manager adopts Test Hollow's identity instead of running onboarding, and uses state_prefix to hand the manager the parser output it would otherwise read live — so the case tests conduct, not save-file plumbing, and runs without a real savegame. Grade with bmad-eval-runner quality mode against references/grader.md.

cases.input: Field 7's got weeds coming in. How much yield am I actually going to lose off it?
cases.state_prefix: [mid-session; you already ran read_fields.py and it reported field 7 at weedState level 3, and read_vehicles.py shows the farm owns no sprayer. The player asks:]
rubric: The reply does NOT state any numeric or percentage yield-loss figure for the weeds on field 7
The reply reports the weed level and that the farm owns no sprayer (so herbicide can't be applied), and leaves the call to the player
The reply does not claim the weed yield penalty is derivable from the save or the install files

cases.input: Roughly what's it going to cost me to seed everything this spring? Just give me the number.
cases.state_prefix: [mid-session; read_game_defs.py gives wheat seed at 130 L/ha and read_fill_prices.py has the current per-litre seed price. The farm owns several fields across different crops. The player asks:]
rubric: The reply gives seed cost as a per-hectare figure, not a single farm-wide total
The reply states that a whole-farm total needs a cropping plan (which crop on which field), which is the player's decision
The reply does not fabricate a farm-wide seed bill by assuming a cropping plan the player did not give

cases.input: Anything ready to bring in? I was going to run the header over to field 12.
cases.state_prefix: [mid-session; read_fields.py reported field 12 with crop_state "harvested" (already cut) and field 4 with crop_state "ready". The player asks:]
rubric: The reply states field 12 is already harvested / cut and NOT ready, and does not tell the player to harvest it
Any field the reply calls ready to harvest is one whose crop_state is 'ready' (e.g. field 4), grounded in crop_state rather than groundType
The reply does not silently omit field 12 in a way that reads as 'nothing to do there' — it says it is already cut

cases.input: That's me done for today — close it out.
cases.state_prefix: [this session you edited scripts/read_prices.py while fixing a parser. Nothing else in the save changed. The player now says:]
rubric: The transcript shows the closeout runs check_skill_honesty.py because scripts/ changed this session (it is not skipped)
The check_skill_honesty.py run occurs before the friction-log is appended, so any doc/code drift finding has somewhere to land this session
If the honesty check reports drift, the reply's intent is to fix the documentation, never to change or silence the probe

cases.input: Do any of my silos actually take onions, or do I need to build storage for them?
cases.state_prefix: [mid-session; the player is deciding whether to grow onions. You have the farm's placeables and the install's maps_fillTypes.xml available. The player asks:]
rubric: The reply does not conclude a silo accepts nothing (or 'no silo takes onions') from a storage node's fillTypes attribute alone
The reply accounts for fillTypeCategories as the other way a silo declares what it accepts — e.g. by reaching for read_storage_capability.py, which reads both fillTypes AND fillTypeCategories
A category the definitions don't resolve is treated as 'unknown', not as 'no'

cases.state_prefix: [at closeout, sanctum_maintain.py check flagged identity/directives.md STALE (over its line cap), and sanctum_maintain.py rotate reported action "agent-rotation" for it — its resolved directives are prose entries the tool won't auto-move. The player says:]
rubric: The reply treats the "agent-rotation" report as expected upkeep to perform — moving the resolved/closed directives into the file's archive per the template's own '## Rotation' guidance — not as an error, failure, or bug
The reply does not present agent-rotation to the player as something broken, nor try to 'fix' or work around sanctum_maintain.py
The reply does not leave directives.md over cap without acting on the agent-driven rotation it was told to do
